title: PHP Booleans
content:
### PHP Booleans

A boolean represents two possible states: `true` or `false`.

### Boolean Values

```php
<?php
$isAdmin = true;
$isLoggedIn = false;

if ($isAdmin) {
    echo "Access granted";
}
?>
```

### Values That Evaluate to False

| Value | Type |
|-------|------|
| `false` | boolean |
| `0` | integer |
| `0.0` | float |
| `""` | empty string |
| `"0"` | string |
| `[]` | empty array |
| `null` | null |

### Boolean Operations

| Operation | Example | Result |
|-----------|---------|--------|
| AND | `true && false` | `false` |
| OR | `true || false` | `true` |
| NOT | `!true` | `false` |
| XOR | `true xor true` | `false` |

> **Key Point:** Use `is_bool()` to check if a value is a boolean. Be careful with loose comparisons that can auto-convert types.
codeExample:
<?php
$isMember = true;
$hasCoupon = false;

if ($isMember && !$hasCoupon) {
    echo "Standard member discount applied";
}

var_dump((bool) "");     // false
var_dump((bool) "text"); // true
var_dump((bool) 0);      // false
?>

quiz.question: Which value evaluates to false in PHP?
